channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police: Colorado woman with 7 kids pointed gun at another driver in road rage incident
item.description: A woman was arrested after she allegedly pointed a gun at another driver in Commerce City on Thursday while seven kids were in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Late car payments hit highest level in decades
item.description: Americans are falling behind on their monthly car payments at the highest rate in more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Violent and sudden. What a firing squad execution looked like through my eyes
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — I've now watched through glass and bars as 11 men were put to death at a South Carolina prison. None of the previous 10 prepared me for watching the firing squad death of Brad Sigmon on Friday night. I might now be unique among U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: Tribes, Native American students file lawsuit over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students filed a lawsuit Friday against the Trump administration, accusing it of failing its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools last month.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A South Carolina man executed by firing squad is the first US prisoner killed this way in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina man who killed his ex-girlfriend’s parents with a baseball bat was executed by firing squad Friday, the first U.S. prisoner in 15 years to die by that method, which he saw as preferable to the electric chair or lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: CDC launches 'conflicts of interest' page about vaccine advisory panel
item.description: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) published a page on Friday listing the conflicts of interest reported by members of a key vaccine advisory committee.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Chick-fil-A introduces new spring menu items, brings back fan-favorite sandwich
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the arrival of spring with new seasonal menu items, including a returning fan favorite.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Convicted double murderer executed by firing squad in South Carolina
item.description: A South Carolina man convicted of brutally murdering a couple in 2001 was executed by firing squad Friday, becoming the first U.S. prisoner to die by the method in 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa causes of death announced
item.description: The causes of death for actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were released Friday after days of mystery and speculation.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Moon mission goes sideways: Private lunar lander declared dead after botched landing
item.description: A private lunar lander is no longer working after landing sideways in a crater near the moon’s south pole and its mission is over, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: By MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, KRON4

item.title: 20 Dem AGs sue over Trump efforts to fire probationary employees
item.description: Democratic state attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 19 states, including California, sued the Trump administration late Thursday over its efforts to carry out mass firings of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Bragg to Liberty and back again: Ceremony to rechristen Army post once named for a Confederate
item.description: FORT BRAGG, N.C. (AP) — The short-lived existence of Fort Liberty came to an end Friday when the nation’s largest Army installation officially returned to its former name: Fort Bragg. Christened a century ago in honor of Confederate Gen.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN BREED and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at Indiana post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CEO of Jack Daniel's responds after Tennessee whiskey pulled from Canada's shelves
item.description: Lawson Whiting, CEO of Brown-Forman, which makes Jack Daniel's, called the move "frustrating" during an earnings call.
item.dc:creator: Nikki McGee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order on student loan forgiveness program
item.description: As part of the program, those working for the government or a non-profit organization have their loans forgiven.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: 12 indicted migrants allegedly assaulted officers at South Texas ICE lockup
item.description: A federal grand jury in South Texas has indicted 10 Mexican nationals and two men from Guatemala on charges of assaulting two correctional officers, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei announced.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: 2 more arrested in theft of bulldog puppies from Colorado store
item.description: Deputies arrested the man who allegedly snatched a pair of bulldog puppies from an enclosure at a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Flight attendant pleads guilty to secretly recording a 14-year-old girl in plane bathroom
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A former American Airlines flight attendant has pleaded guilty to secretly recording video of a 14-year-old girl using an airplane bathroom and having recordings of four other girls using the lavatories.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: War heroes, a bomber and military firsts among 26,000 images flagged for removal in Pentagon's DEI purge
item.description: Photos seemed to be flagged for removal simply because their file included the word ”gay,” including an image of the B-29 aircraft Enola Gay, which dropped the first atomic bomb on Hiroshima, Japan, during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Debanking — the closure of accounts that banks consider risky, often with little notice or explanation — has become a key issue for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: A San Carlos Apache teen who was reported missing and then found dead marks one of the latest tragedies as Native American communities continue to grapple with high rates of people being killed or going missing.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge rules Trump illegally removed labor board member
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday ruled that President Trump unlawfully removed a Democratic member of the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB), suggesting in her ruling that the president sought to test the limits of his power in doing so. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump stresses Cabinet secretaries lead agency cuts, but keeps Musk as threat
item.description: President Trump on Thursday stressed that his Cabinet secretaries take the lead on making cuts to their agencies and staff, insisting that officials are empowered to make decisions before Elon Musk can step in. “I want the Cabinet members, go first, keep all the people you want.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho court releases surviving roommates' text messages from night of student murders
item.description: Text messages between two surviving roommates of four university students killed in a home invasion stabbing have been revealed for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs tariff exemptions for certain imports from Canada, Mexico
item.description: President Trump on Thursday signed off on tariff exemptions for imports from Canada and Mexico that are covered under a 2020 North American trade agreement, marking the latest reversal of duties imposed earlier this week. Under the orders, goods compliant with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk's PAC is running ads touting Trump's accomplishments
item.description: The political action committee that Elon Musk funded to the tune of more than $200 million on behalf of President Donald Trump launched a television advertisement this week lauding the first weeks of the administration in which the Tesla CEO is serving.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump wants to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has said he wants his new education chief, Linda McMahon, to “put herself out of a job” and close the Education Department. McMahon was confirmed by the Senate on Monday, and an executive order to shutter the department could come as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA and COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
